docs.title: Wave 1 — Workflow fixes (30 min)
docs.text:
Files: .github/workflows/pypi-publish.yml, .github/workflows/ci.yml

Changes: 1. pypi-publish.yml: insert new step before pip wheel: 

- name: Upgrade setuptools (PEP 621 support)
  run: pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
2. ci.yml: in the python job, before pip install pytest: 
- run: pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
3. (Already done in v0.4.10, verify) All workflows pin ubuntu-22.04, not ubuntu-latest. 4. (Already done in v0.4.9, verify) pypi-publish.yml cp target is bindings/python/par_runtime/lib/.

Verification: - dune build clean - Local pip wheel . --no-deps in bindings/python/ produces par_runtime-0.4.10-py3-none-any.whl (correct name, since dune-project still says 0.4.10 at this point) - unzip -l shows par_runtime/lib/par_capi.so

Commit message: fix(ci): upgrade setuptools before pip wheel (PAR-cog prevention)

docs.title: Wave 2 — Acceptance test script (1-2 hours)
docs.text:
Files: NEW scripts/release-acceptance-test.py

Specification: - Python 3 script, no external deps (uses only stdlib + the wheel under test) - CLI: release-acceptance-test.py <wheel-path> <expected-version> - Returns exit 0 on success, non-zero on failure - Clear stdout for human reading, machine-parseable stderr for CI

Test levels (in order, fail-fast):

LEVEL 1 (MUST pass):
  - pip install --no-cache-dir <wheel-path>  (in fresh venv)
  - python -c "import par_runtime"
  - python -c "assert par_runtime.__version__ == '<expected-version>'"

LEVEL 2 (SHOULD pass):
  - python -c "from par_runtime import Runtime"
  - python -c "
      rt = Runtime(TEST_CONFIG)
      rt.register_tool('echo', 'Echo', '{\"type\":\"object\"}')
      rt.close()
    "
  - where TEST_CONFIG is the validated config from test_runtime.py:14-45

LEVEL 3 (informational, never fails the build):
  - python -c "print(par_runtime.__all__)"  (log exports)
  - python -c "import ctypes; ctypes.CDLL(par_runtime._ffi._find_library())"  (log .so path)

Skeleton code in this ROADMAP §"Reference Implementation" below.

Verification: - Run locally: python3 scripts/release-acceptance-test.py /tmp/par_runtime-0.4.10-py3-none-any.whl 0.4.10 (should fail because that wheel is broken — confirms script catches bugs) - Run locally: python3 scripts/release-acceptance-test.py <locally-built-good-wheel> 0.4.10 (should pass — confirms script doesn't have false positives)

Commit message: feat(release): add release-acceptance-test.py end-to-end install test

docs.title: Wave 3 — Acceptance test workflow (1-2 hours)
docs.text:
Files: NEW .github/workflows/release-acceptance.yml

Specification: - Trigger: workflow_run on PyPI publish workflow completed successfully - Matrix: 3 containers (debian:12, ubuntu:22.04, ubuntu:24.04) - Each container: - Install Python 3 (apt-get install python3 python3-pip python3-venv) - Download wheel from GH Release artifacts (via gh release download or actions/download-artifact) - Run python3 scripts/release-acceptance-test.py <wheel> <version> - Aggregate: all 3 must pass - Output: if any fails, post comment on release tag with failure details (uses actions/github-script)

Skeleton yaml in this ROADMAP §"Reference Implementation" below.

Verification: - Manually trigger workflow on existing v0.4.10 tag → should FAIL (proves it catches bugs) - After Wave 1+2 land, trigger again on dummy tag → should PASS

Commit message: feat(release): add release-acceptance.yml 3-platform matrix gate

docs.title: Wave 4 — Local dry-run script (30 min)
docs.text:
Files: NEW scripts/release-local-test.sh

Specification: - Bash script, requires Docker locally - Builds wheel locally (calls make install-dev + pip wheel) - For each platform in matrix: - docker run --rm -v $(pwd):/work <image> bash -c "cd /work && python3 scripts/release-acceptance-test.py <wheel> <version>" - Reports pass/fail per platform

Verification: - bash scripts/release-local-test.sh runs end-to-end on local Debian 12 dev box - Uses local Docker (or skipped if Docker unavailable, with clear error)

Commit message: feat(release): add release-local-test.sh Docker-based dry-run

docs.title: Wave 5 — Documentation (30 min)
docs.text:
Files: docs/rules/release.md

Changes: Add new section after "Release Flow":

## End-to-End Release Test (MANDATORY before any PyPI upload)

Per postmortem 2026-06-21 (3 consecutive P0s in v0.4.8/9/10), every release
MUST pass end-to-end install test on representative target platforms BEFORE
any twine upload.

### Local dry-run (fast iteration)

[...]

### CI acceptance workflow (final gate)

[...]

### Manual matrix verification (post-PyPI upload)

[...]

Commit message: docs(release): document E2E test procedure per v0.4.11 postmortem

docs.title: Wave 6 — Bump to v0.4.11-beta (15 min)
docs.text:
Commands: 

make release-beta BUMP_TYPE=patch FORCE=1
# Result: 0.4.10 → 0.4.11-beta.YYYYMMDD
git add -A && git commit -m "chore(version): bump 0.4.10 → 0.4.11-beta.YYYYMMDD"
git tag v0.4.11-beta.YYYYMMDD
git push origin main --tags

Wait for CI: - ci.yml on main push (beta version, no tag) → ~5 min - release.yml + pypi-publish.yml + opam-publish.yml on tag push → ~5-8 min each - release-acceptance.yml triggers after pypi-publish → ~3-5 min

GATE: ALL 5 workflows must be green. If acceptance fails, do NOT proceed — fix and re-tag beta.

docs.title: Wave 7 — Manual twine upload (10 min, after all CI green)
docs.text:
Commands: 

# Download the wheel that CI built (now proven good by acceptance workflow)
gh release download v0.4.11-beta.YYYYMMDD --repo jcz2020/par --pattern 'par_runtime-*.whl' --dir /tmp/

# Upload to PyPI
twine upload --non-interactive /tmp/par_runtime-0.4.11_beta.*-py3-none-any.whl

Note: PyPI doesn't allow _beta in version. The actual filename uses - (dash) not _. The version string 0.4.11-beta.20260621 becomes filename par_runtime-0.4.11_beta.20260621-py3-none-any.whl (pip normalizes - to _).

Actually wait — twine/PyPI may reject 0.4.11-beta.YYYYMMDD because PyPI normalizes names. Test with beta upload first. If it works, proceed.

GATE: twine upload succeeds, https://pypi.org/project/par-runtime/0.4.11b.YYYYMMDD/ exists.

docs.title: Wave 8 — Beta soak (24 hours, optional)
docs.text:
• Announce beta in commit message of stable tag
  • (Optional) Real-machine install on Debian 12 + Ubuntu 22.04 LTS for 24 hours
  • If any user reports issues, fix and re-tag beta

If time-constrained, can skip soak and go straight to stable.

docs.title: Wave 9 — Strip beta → v0.4.11 stable (15 min)
docs.text:
Commands: 

sed -i 's/"0\.4\.11-beta\.[0-9]*"/"0.4.11"/' dune-project
make sync-version
make install-dev
# Update CHANGES.md: change "v0.4.11-beta.YYYYMMDD" → "v0.4.11"
git add -A && git commit -m "release: v0.4.11 — release engineering fix (PAR-0qf/8cs/cog)"
git tag -a v0.4.11 -m "Release v0.4.11 — release engineering fix"
git push origin main --tags

Wait for CI: - All 5 workflows run again on the stable tag - release-acceptance.yml MUST pass before twine upload

docs.title: Wave 10 — Stable twine upload (10 min)
docs.text:
Same as Wave 7 but for par_runtime-0.4.11-py3-none-any.whl: 

gh release download v0.4.11 --repo jcz2020/par --pattern 'par_runtime-*.whl' --dir /tmp/
twine upload --non-interactive /tmp/par_runtime-0.4.11-py3-none-any.whl

docs.title: Wave 11 — Final verification (30 min)
docs.text:
Real-machine matrix install (not CI, not Docker — actual target OS):

Platform Method Expected Debian 12 (current dev box) pip install par-runtime==0.4.11 + python -c "import par_runtime" works Debian 12 (current dev box) full Runtime init + register_tool + close works (If available) Ubuntu 22.04 LTS same works (If available) Ubuntu 24.04 LTS same works macOS arm64 install.sh + par --version 0.4.11

docs.title: Wave 12 — Cleanup (15 min)
docs.text:
• [ ] Close bd PAR-0qf, PAR-8cs, PAR-cog, PAR-j8i
  • [ ] Update bd PAR-b94 PyPI portion → CLOSED, opam portion still OPEN
  • [ ] bd dolt push
  • [ ] Update README banner: change "broken until v0.5.0" → "fixed in v0.4.11"
  • [ ] (User action) Yank par-runtime==0.4.8 + ==0.4.9 on PyPI if not already done
  • [ ] (User action) Delete broken wheel assets from v0.4.8/9/10 GH Releases

docs.title: scripts/release-acceptance-test.py
docs.text:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""End-to-end install test for par-runtime wheel.

Usage: release-acceptance-test.py <wheel-path> <expected-version>

Exit codes:
  0: all tests passed
  1: Level 1 failed (import / version mismatch) — BLOCKING
  2: Level 2 failed (Runtime init / tool registration) — BLOCKING
  3: invalid arguments
"""
import json
import subprocess
import sys
import venv
from pathlib import Path


TEST_CONFIG = json.dumps({
    "persistence": ["Sqlite", ":memory:"],
    "event_bus": {
        "buffer_capacity": 10,
        "delivery": {
            "max_delivery_attempts": 3,
            "initial_retry_delay": 0.1,
            "retry_backoff": ["Fixed", 0.5],
            "delivery_timeout": 5.0,
        },
        "dlq_enabled": False,
        "critical_event_types": [],
    },
    "default_quota": {
        "max_concurrent_tasks": 4,
        "max_concurrent_tools_per_agent": 2,
        "max_tokens_per_turn": None,
        "max_total_tokens": None,
    },
    "shutdown": {
        "drain_timeout": 5.0,
        "cancel_grace_period": 2.0,
        "flush_batch_size": 100,
    },
    "llm_providers": [],
    "eval_limits": {"max_depth": 10, "max_node_visits": 1000},
    "parallel_tool_execution": True,
})


def run_in_venv(venv_python: str, code: str) -> tuple[int, str, str]:
    """Run Python code in venv, return (exit_code, stdout, stderr)."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        [venv_python, "-c", code],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    return result.returncode, result.stdout, result.stderr


def main():
    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
        print(f"Usage: {sys.argv[0]} <wheel-path> <expected-version>", file=sys.stderr)
        return 3

    wheel_path = Path(sys.argv[1]).resolve()
    expected_version = sys.argv[2]

    if not wheel_path.exists():
        print(f"FAIL: wheel not found at {wheel_path}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 3

    print(f"Testing wheel: {wheel_path.name}")
    print(f"Expected version: {expected_version}")
    print(f"Wheel size: {wheel_path.stat().st_size:,} bytes")
    print()

    # Create fresh venv
    venv_dir = Path("/tmp/par-acceptance-venv")
    if venv_dir.exists():
        import shutil
        shutil.rmtree(venv_dir)
    print(f"Creating venv at {venv_dir}...")
    venv.create(venv_dir, with_pip=True, clear=True)
    venv_python = str(venv_dir / "bin" / "python")
    venv_pip = str(venv_dir / "bin" / "pip")

    # Install wheel
    print(f"\n=== Installing wheel ===")
    result = subprocess.run(
        [venv_pip, "install", "--no-cache-dir", str(wheel_path)],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"FAIL: pip install failed", file=sys.stderr)
        print(result.stderr, file=sys.stderr)
        return 1
    print(f"PASS: pip install")

    # Level 1: import + version
    print(f"\n=== Level 1: import + version (MUST pass) ===")
    code = f"import par_runtime; assert par_runtime.__version__ == '{expected_version}', f'version mismatch: {{par_runtime.__version__}} != {expected_version}'; print('import ok, version', par_runtime.__version__)"
    rc, out, err = run_in_venv(venv_python, code)
    if rc != 0:
        print(f"FAIL: Level 1", file=sys.stderr)
        print(f"stdout: {out}", file=sys.stderr)
        print(f"stderr: {err}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1
    print(f"PASS: {out.strip()}")

    # Level 2: Runtime init + tool registration + close
    print(f"\n=== Level 2: Runtime init + register_tool + close (SHOULD pass) ===")
    code = f"""
import json
from par_runtime import Runtime
config = {TEST_CONFIG!r}
rt = Runtime(config)
rt.register_tool('echo', 'Echo tool', '{{"type": "object"}}')
rt.close()
print('Runtime lifecycle ok')
"""
    rc, out, err = run_in_venv(venv_python, code)
    if rc != 0:
        print(f"FAIL: Level 2", file=sys.stderr)
        print(f"stdout: {out}", file=sys.stderr)
        print(f"stderr: {err}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    print(f"PASS: {out.strip()}")

    # Level 3: informational
    print(f"\n=== Level 3: informational ===")
    code = "import par_runtime; print('exports:', par_runtime.__all__)"
    rc, out, _ = run_in_venv(venv_python, code)
    print(out.strip())

    code = "from par_runtime._ffi import _find_library; print('so path:', _find_library())"
    rc, out, _ = run_in_venv(venv_python, code)
    print(out.strip())

    print(f"\n=== ALL TESTS PASSED ===")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

docs.title: .github/workflows/release-acceptance.yml
docs.text:
name: Release acceptance test

# Triggers AFTER pypi-publish.yml completes (wheel is on GH Release).
# If this workflow fails, the wheel MUST NOT be uploaded to PyPI.

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["PyPI publish"]
    types: [completed]

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  accept:
    name: Accept on ${{ matrix.container }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: ${{ matrix.container }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        container:
          - debian:12
          - ubuntu:22.04
          - ubuntu:24.04
    if: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install Python
        run: |
          apt-get update -qq
          apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip python3-venv curl
          python3 --version

      - name: Download wheel from GH Release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}"
          echo "Downloading wheel from tag $TAG"
          curl -fsSL -o /tmp/wheel.whl \
            "https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/$TAG/par_runtime-$TAG-py3-none-any.whl" || true
          # The filename may have dashes (version normalization); fallback to globbing
          if [ ! -f /tmp/wheel.whl ]; then
            gh release download "$TAG" --repo "${{ github.repository }}" --pattern 'par_runtime-*.whl' --dir /tmp/
            mv /tmp/par_runtime-*.whl /tmp/wheel.whl
          fi
          ls -la /tmp/wheel.whl
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Run acceptance test
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}"
          # Strip leading 'v' from tag, replace dashes appropriately for version
          VERSION="${TAG#v}"
          python3 scripts/release-acceptance-test.py /tmp/wheel.whl "$VERSION"

  gate:
    name: All acceptance tests passed
    needs: accept
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Check matrix result
        run: |
          echo "Matrix conclusion: ${{ needs.accept.result }}"
          if [ "${{ needs.accept.result }}" != "success" ]; then
            echo "FAIL: release acceptance test failed on at least one platform"
            echo "DO NOT upload to PyPI until this is green"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "PASS: all platforms accepted the wheel"
          echo "Next step: manual twine upload (see docs/rules/release.md)"

docs.title: scripts/release-local-test.sh
docs.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Local dry-run of the release acceptance test.
# Requires Docker. Builds wheel locally, then runs acceptance test in each container.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not available. Install Docker or use CI workflow instead."
    exit 1
fi

# 1. Build wheel locally
echo "=== Building wheel locally ==="
make install-dev >/dev/null
mkdir -p bindings/python/par_runtime/lib
cp -f _build/default/lib/ffi/par_capi.so bindings/python/par_runtime/lib/
(cd bindings/python && rm -rf dist && pip wheel . -w dist/ --no-deps)
WHEEL=$(ls bindings/python/dist/par_runtime-*.whl | head -1)
VERSION=$(sed -n 's/^(version "\([^"]*\)").*/\1/p' dune-project)
echo "Wheel: $WHEEL"
echo "Version: $VERSION"

# 2. Run acceptance test in each container
CONTAINERS=(debian:12 ubuntu:22.04 ubuntu:24.04)
ALL_PASS=true

for c in "${CONTAINERS[@]}"; do
    echo ""
    echo "=== Testing on $c ==="
    if docker run --rm \
        -v "$PWD:/work" \
        -w /work \
        "$c" \
        bash -c "apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip python3-venv > /dev/null && python3 scripts/release-acceptance-test.py /work/$WHEEL $VERSION"; then
        echo "PASS: $c"
    else
        echo "FAIL: $c"
        ALL_PASS=false
    fi
done

echo ""
if $ALL_PASS; then
    echo "=== ALL PLATFORMS PASSED ==="
    exit 0
else
    echo "=== ONE OR MORE PLATFORMS FAILED ==="
    exit 1
fi

docs.title: Risk Assessment
docs.text: Risk Probability Impact Mitigation release-acceptance.yml workflow_run trigger doesn't fire on fork PRs Medium Medium Use tag push trigger instead; document the limitation PyPI rejects 0.4.11-beta.YYYYMMDD (some chars not allowed) Low Medium Test beta upload first; PyPI allows alphanumerics + . + - in version per PEP 440 ubuntu-22.04 image gets retired while we depend on it Low (years away) High Document upgrade path; v0.5.0 manylinux removes dependency on ubuntu version entirely Acceptance workflow runs slow (5 min per platform × 3 = 15 min gate) Medium Low Use fail-fast: false so we get all results in parallel; document expected time Docker not available on user's dev box (for local dry-run script) Low Low Script detects + suggests using CI workflow instead GitHub Actions workflow_run event payload missing head_branch for tag Low Medium Fallback to parsing release URL or tag from workflow_run head_sha

docs.title: Estimated Effort
docs.text:
Wave Effort Notes 0 (pre-flight) 15 min Reading docs 1 (workflow fixes) 30 min 2 yaml edits 2 (acceptance test script) 1-2 hours Python script 3 (acceptance test workflow) 1-2 hours yaml + matrix design 4 (local dry-run script) 30 min Bash script 5 (documentation) 30 min Markdown 6 (beta bump + push) 15 min + CI wait Wait ~10 min for all 5 workflows 7 (beta twine upload) 10 min First PyPI interaction 8 (beta soak) 0-24 hours Optional 9 (strip beta + push) 15 min + CI wait Wait ~10 min 10 (stable twine upload) 10 min 11 (final verification) 30 min Real-machine tests 12 (cleanup) 15 min bd updates Total (no soak) 6-8 hours Total (with soak) 30-32 hours Mostly waiting

Single-session completion feasible if beta soak skipped.

docs.title: Open Questions (need answer before Wave 6)
docs.text:
1. Beta soak duration: skip (go straight to stable) or wait 24 hours?
  2. Yank previous broken versions: user yank 0.4.8 + 0.4.9 manually before v0.4.11 stable? Or after?
  3. GH Release asset cleanup: user delete broken wheels from v0.4.8/9/10 releases? Or leave as historical record?
  4. Acceptance workflow trigger: workflow_run (after PyPI publish workflow) or direct push: tags (parallel to PyPI publish)? The former is more correct but newer GitHub feature.

docs.title: Reference Documents
docs.text:
• docs/release-pipeline-redesign.md — full postmortem + scope split (v0.4.11 MVP + v0.5+ stretch)
  • docs/rules/release.md — existing release rules (Wave 5 updates this)
  • docs/STRATEGY.md — §5 release strategy, §10 beta-first (Wave 6+9 follow this)
  • CHANGES.md — v0.4.11 entry prepared in Wave 5/9

End of v0.4.11 ROADMAP. Next session: start from Wave 0.

docs.title: v0.4.8 Roadmap — Structured Output (Runtime.invoke_structured)
docs.text: Status: Draft v1. Created 2026-06-19. Theme: Single feature — typed structured LLM output via JSON Schema. Provider-native (OpenAI response_format, Anthropic tool_use hack) with graceful fallback for other providers. Type: Feature. Minor-equivalent bump from v0.4.7 (would be v0.5.0 under strict SemVer; staying in v0.4.x per project convention until v0.5 theme emerges). Predecessor: v0.4.7 (hotfix — ignore hallucinated tool_calls). Source issues: PAR-xd5 (P0), PAR-5cc (P1). Strategic alignment: STRATEGY.md §4 priority #1 = 类型严谨. This is the strongest differentiator vs Python frameworks (LangChain/PydanticAI run-time crashes → PAR compile-time + schema-validated LLM output).

docs.title: Rationale
docs.text:
Today, Runtime.invoke returns free-text LLM output. Users who need structured data must:

  1. Hand-write a system prompt instructing the LLM to "respond in JSON",
  2. Strip markdown fences (json ... ) themselves,
  3. Call Yojson.Safe.from_string and hope the LLM obeyed,
  4. Manually validate the shape.

Every step is a foot-gun. Worse, PAR doesn't use the native structured-output APIs of its providers — OpenAI's response_format: { type: "json_schema", ... } (Aug 2024) and Anthropic's tool_use synthesis are both unsupported. STRATEGY.md §4 priority #1 is type rigor; an OCaml LLM SDK without first-class structured output undercuts the core value proposition.

v0.4.8 closes the gap with one new public API: Runtime.invoke_structured. It is provider-aware (uses native structured output where available, falls back to prompt+validate elsewhere), runs an in-band retry loop on schema violations, and reuses PAR's existing JSON Schema validator (added v0.4.0 for tool output validation).

docs.title: Primary entry point
docs.text:
(* lib/core/runtime.mli — new val after line 80 *)

val invoke_structured :
  runtime ->
  agent_id:string ->
  message:string ->
  response_schema:Yojson.Safe.t ->       (* JSON Schema describing desired output *)
  ?max_repair_attempts:int ->            (* default 3; feedback retries on violation *)
  ?cancellation_token:cancellation_token ->
  ?conversation:conversation ->           (* resume an existing conversation *)
  ?on_tool_event:(event -> unit) ->
  unit ->
  (structured_invoke_result, error_category * conversation) result

docs.title: Return type
docs.text:
(* lib/core/types.ml — new type near invoke_result at line 140-143 *)

type structured_invoke_result = {
  value        : Yojson.Safe.t;   (* schema-validated JSON *)
  raw_response : llm_response;    (* original LLM reply (for debugging / token accounting) *)
  conversation : conversation;    (* updated, includes any repair messages *)
  attempts     : int;             (* 1 = happy path; >1 = repairs happened *)
} [@@deriving yojson]

Mirrors invoke_result shape (types.ml:140-143) so callers can chain ?conversation across calls — same convention as Runtime.invoke.

docs.title: Why generic Yojson.Safe.t (not typed 'a)
docs.text: A higher-order invoke_structured_t ~decode:(Yojson.Safe.t -> ('a, _) result) was considered. Deferred to v0.5 — the generic API + caller-side [%of_yojson: T] covers v0.4.8 use cases without polymorphic-record gymnastics that complicate the FFI surface.

docs.title: Convenience pattern for callers
docs.text:
type person = { name : string; age : int } [@@deriving yojson]

let get_person rt ~message =
  let schema = `Assoc [
    ("type", `String "object");
    ("properties", `Assoc [
      ("name", `Assoc [("type", `String "string")]);
      ("age",  `Assoc [("type", `String "integer")]);
    ]);
    ("required", `List [`String "name"; `String "age"]);
  ] in
  match Runtime.invoke_structured rt ~agent_id:"default" ~message ~response_schema:schema with
  | Error (cat, _) -> Error (Runtime.string_of_error_category cat)
  | Ok { value; _ } ->
    match Person.of_yojson value with
    | Ok p -> Ok p
    | Error e -> Error ("PPX decode failed: " ^ e)

docs.title: Provider Strategy Matrix
docs.text:
UPDATE 2026-06-19 (post-librarian research): Anthropic now has native structured output via output_config.format (GA on Opus 4.5–4.8, Sonnet 4.5–4.6, Haiku 4.5). This replaces the originally-planned tool_use synthesis hack and simplifies WU-4.

Provider Strategy Implementation Effort OpenAI Native response_format Add response_format: { type: "json_schema", json_schema: { name, schema, strict: true } } to build_request_body (openai_provider.ml:89-112) 0.5 day Anthropic Native output_config.format (2026 GA) Add output_config: { format: { type: "json_schema", schema: <user_schema> } } to build_request_body (anthropic_provider.ml:109-136). Parse JSON from content[0].text (text block, not tool_use). 0.5 day (was 1d under tool_use plan) Mock Direct JSON emit Test fixtures return canned JSON matching the schema. New mock_provider.ml field. 0.5 day Ollama / Custom Prompt directive + lenient parse + local validate Inject "You must respond with valid JSON matching this schema: ..." into system prompt. Strip markdown fences. Parse. Validate via Validation.validate_tool_input_result. 0.5 day

Decision: Native providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) self-register structured capability via the new complete_structured_fn field on llm_service. Generic fallback covers everything else — no provider is rejected. Mock implements structured for deterministic tests.

docs.title: Architecture: new llm_service field (Strategy B — additive, non-breaking)
docs.text:
(* lib/core/types.ml:685-691 — extend llm_service record *)

type llm_service = {
  complete_fn : ...;                                            (* unchanged *)
  stream_fn   : ...;                                            (* unchanged *)
  close_fn    : unit -> unit;                                   (* unchanged *)
  (* NEW in v0.4.8 *)
  complete_structured_fn :
    (model_config -> tool_descriptor list -> conversation ->
     Yojson.Safe.t ->                                          (* response_schema *)
     (llm_response, error_category) result) option;            (* None = fallback path *)
}

Why this approach (not ?response_format on complete): - Non-breaking: existing custom providers (docs/howto/custom-llm-provider.md) keep compiling — they just don't get native structured support. - Capability self-declares: Runtime.invoke_structured checks complete_structured_fn = Some _ to pick native vs fallback path. No provider-variant sniffing on model_config.provider. - Matches existing pattern: event_bus_service (types.ml:669-675) uses the same "function-record as service" style.

docs.title: Reuse: existing in-tree validator
docs.text:
lib/core/validation.ml already has a partial JSON Schema validator added in v0.4.0:

  • validate_value : Yojson.t -> Yojson.t -> string list -> (unit, validation_error) result (validation.ml:107) — recursive walker
  • validate_tool_input : Yojson.t -> Yojson.t -> (unit, validation_error) result (validation.ml:162) — top-level entry; checks type, required, properties
  • validate_tool_input_result : Yojson.t -> Yojson.t -> (unit, error_category) result (validation.ml:199) — wraps as Invalid_input

Already used at engine.ml:104-114 for register_tool ~output_schema validation. Clone this exact call pattern for structured output.

docs.title: v0.4.8 limitation (documented honestly per Oracle D4)
docs.text:
The in-tree validator is more limited than "1-level nesting" suggests:

  • validate_value checks type/enum/min/max/length at ONE path
  • validate_tool_input recurses into properties.X via validate_value — BUT does NOT recurse into nested properties or items
  • Consequence: "1-level for objects, 0-level for arrays". A schema {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}} is validated only as "is it an array" — element types unchecked.

v0.4.8 scope: Top-level object properties validated (type/required/enum/bounds). Array items, nested object properties, and oneOf/anyOf are NOT validated in the fallback path. Native providers (OpenAI/Anthropic) validate deeply server-side — this limitation only affects Ollama/Custom fallback. Deferred to v0.5: either extend validate_value to recurse properly (and handle items, oneOf, $ref), or add jsonschema opam dependency.

Documentation requirement (WU-8): SDK reference must state this honestly. Example callout: "The fallback path validates top-level object properties only. For nested objects or arrays, use OpenAI or Anthropic providers which validate server-side."

docs.title: The gap
docs.text:
The existing Retry middleware (lib/middleware/retry.ml) wraps the on_error hook, but on_error : error_category -> handler_result option (types.ml:215) does not receive conversation — it cannot append a repair message to the conversation. Confirmed: zero matches for feedback|retry_with|repair in lib/.

Implication: structured-output repair cannot be implemented as middleware. It must be a local loop in Engine.

docs.title: New Engine.run_structured helper
docs.text:
(* lib/core/engine.ml — new function near run_agent (after line 204) *)

(* Pseudocode — actual implementation in WU-2 *)
let rec run_structured
    ~response_schema:Yojson.Safe.t
    ~max_repair_attempts:int
    ~(complete_fn : ... -> Yojson.Safe.t -> (llm_response, error_category) result) (* native or fallback *)
    ~agent ~conv ...
    attempt =
  (* 1. Make the LLM call (native structured or fallback with prompt directive *)
  let result = complete_fn agent.Model.model agent.tools conv response_schema in
  (match result with
   | Error cat -> Error (cat, conv)  (* LLM/network error → no retry, propagate *)
   | Ok llm_resp ->
     (* 2. Extract JSON from response (handles ```json fences for fallback path *)
     let text = llm_resp.text in
     (match Util.extract_json_from_text text with
      | Error msg ->
        if attempt >= max_repair_attempts
        then Error (Invalid_input ("JSON parse failed after " ^ string_of_int attempt ^ " attempts: " ^ msg, None), conv_with_assistant llm_resp)
        else
          (* 3a. Append assistant reply + user feedback, recurse *)
          let conv' = add_assistant_message conv llm_resp in
          let conv'' = add_user_feedback conv' ("Your previous response was not valid JSON: " ^ msg ^ ". Please respond with valid JSON matching the schema.") in
          run_structured ~response_schema ~max_repair_attempts ~complete_fn ~agent ~conv:conv'' (attempt + 1)
      | Ok json ->
        (* 3b. Validate JSON against schema *)
        (match Validation.validate_tool_input_result response_schema json with
         | Ok () -> Ok ({ value = json; raw_response = llm_resp; conversation = conv_with_assistant conv llm_resp; attempts = attempt })
         | Error cat ->
           if attempt >= max_repair_attempts
           then Error (cat, conv_with_assistant conv llm_resp)
           else
             let conv' = add_assistant_message conv llm_resp in
             let conv'' = add_user_feedback conv' (Printf.sprintf "Schema violation: %s. Please respond with JSON matching this schema." (Runtime.string_of_error_category cat)) in
             run_structured ~response_schema ~max_repair_attempts ~complete_fn ~agent ~conv:conv'' (attempt + 1))))

docs.title: Reuse points
docs.text:
• Engine.add_assistant_message (engine.ml:175) — append LLM reply to conversation
  • New Engine.add_user_feedback — small helper, mirrors add_assistant_message shape
  • Validation.validate_tool_input_result (validation.ml:199) — schema check (reuses v0.4.0 code)
  • drain_into_conv pattern (engine.ml:217-227) — template for mid-loop conversation mutation

docs.title: Bypassing middleware
docs.text:
The structured repair loop is local to Engine.run_structured. It does not flow through on_error. Rationale:

  • Middleware on_error cannot see conversation (signature gap, see above).
  • Structured-output violations are a different failure mode than HTTP/network retry.
  • Keeping the loop local means zero middleware signature changes — fully backward compatible.

Retry policy reuse: when LLM call itself returns Error (Rate_limited | Timeout | External_failure, _), the existing Retry middleware handles it as usual (via Runtime.invoke's normal path). The structured loop only handles post-LLM parse/schema failures.

docs.title: Lenient JSON Extraction (new helper)
docs.text:
LLMs (especially fallback-path providers) often wrap JSON in markdown fences or prose. New helper:

(* lib/core/util.ml or lib/core/json_extract.ml — new module *)

val extract_json_from_text : string -> (Yojson.Safe.t, string) result
(** [extract_json_from_text text] extracts a JSON value from a free-form LLM response.
    - Strips ```json ... ``` and ``` ... ``` fences
    - Finds the first balanced {...} or [...] block
    - Returns [Error msg] if no valid JSON found *)

Implementation sketch: 1. Strip leading/trailing whitespace 2. If starts with ```, strip first line + last line 3. Try Yojson.Safe.from_string directly; on success, return 4. On failure, scan for first { or [, then track depth to find matching close 5. Try Yojson.Safe.from_string on the substring

Tests: 8+ cases covering plain JSON, fenced JSON, JSON in prose, malformed JSON, nested objects, arrays, empty input, multiple JSON blocks (return first).

docs.title: Test Conventions (post-survey, 2026-06-19)
docs.text:
Survey of existing test infrastructure revealed these conventions — new tests MUST follow them:

  1. Provider tests are consolidated in test/test_providers.ml, NOT split per provider. → Create ONE test/test_providers_structured.ml for both OpenAI + Anthropic structured tests.
  2. Engine/Runtime integration tests use test/test_integration.ml pattern (local mock_llm closure + Engine.run_agent direct call). → Create test/test_engine_structured.ml and test/test_runtime_structured.ml.
  3. No shared helpers — copy-paste fixtures (dummy_model, text_response, mock_llm, with_token, basic_agent, make_registry, error_to_string) from test_integration.ml verbatim. Do NOT factor into a shared module.
  4. No HTTP mock infrastructure — provider tests assert on request body shape (build body → Yojson parse → assert keys), NOT on canned HTTP responses. Real-network tests only test error paths (connection refused).
  5. Python tests use unittest.TestCase, NOT pytest fixtures. No conftest.py. Copy _test_config() helper from test_runtime.py:14-45 (uses poly variant encoding ["Sqlite", ":memory:"], NOT {"tag":...}).
  6. Mock_provider extension (lib/providers/mock_provider.ml): currently has Text | With_tool_calls | Error variants. For structured output tests, prefer local closure pattern (option 2 in survey) over extending Mock_provider — matches existing convention. Mock_provider gets a complete_structured_fn = Some _ field like other providers.
  7. test/dune stanzas: every new test file needs a (test (name ...) (libraries par eio_main alcotest ...) (modules ...)) entry. Provider tests need additional eio eio_main tls-eio x509 mirage-crypto-rng.unix.

docs.title: WU-1 · Type additions + API surface — 0.5 day
docs.text:
Files: lib/core/types.ml, lib/core/runtime.mli, lib/core/runtime.ml

Scope: - Add structured_invoke_result record to types.ml near line 140 - Add complete_structured_fn field to llm_service record (types.ml:685-691) — make it option, default None - Update all llm_service constructors (runtime.ml:618-622, mock_provider.ml:138, both providers) to set the field to None initially (WU-3/WU-4/WU-5 will populate with Some _) - Add Runtime.invoke_structured to runtime.mli and stub in runtime.ml (delegates to WU-7 real impl) - Add new Structured_output_completed of { attempts : int; schema_valid : bool; task_id : Task_id.t } event to event variant

Verification (UPDATED post-Oracle BS-3): - dune build clean — covers entire tree including bin/, examples/, test/ (not just lib/). Adding new event variant breaks every exhaustive match without catch-all. - dune runtest — existing 952 tests pass (no behavior change, all new code paths stubbed) - grep -rn "match.*event with" bin/ examples/ test/ lib/ — identify every consumer of event variant; ensure no exhaustive match breaks - lsp_diagnostics clean on all touched files

docs.title: WU-2 · Engine.run_structured with feedback loop — 2 days
docs.text:
UPDATED post-Oracle review (2026-06-19): Oracle flagged 2 must-fix issues — (1) cancellation token not checked between repair attempts, (2) bypassing all middleware creates observability gap. Both addressed below.

Files: lib/core/engine.ml, lib/core/engine.mli, new lib/core/json_extract.ml

Scope: - Implement Engine.run_structured per pseudocode below - Add Engine.add_user_feedback : conversation -> string -> conversation helper (mirrors add_assistant_message at engine.ml:175) - MUST FIX BS-1 — Cancellation check: At the top of each loop iteration, check Cancellation.is_cancelled token. If cancelled, return Error (Timeout, conv). ~3 LOC, prevents unbounded LLM calls on cancelled token. - MUST FIX D2 — Fire middleware hooks: run_structured MUST invoke on_before_llm and on_after_llm middleware hooks (the conversation-aware-but-not-mutating ones) around each LLM call within the repair loop. These hooks already work without signature changes. Rationale: an agent with middleware = [Logging] must have structured calls logged same as regular calls. - For on_error: keep local loop as repair authority (do NOT widen on_error signature — breaking change). Add new ?on_repair_attempt:(int -> error_category -> conversation -> unit) option callback parameter to invoke_structured (purely observational). - Implement Json_extract.extract_json_from_text (WU-6 component, do together) - Wire native vs fallback dispatch: pick llm.complete_structured_fn if Some, else synth fallback closure

Verification: 12+ new unit tests in test/test_engine_structured.ml: - Happy path (1 attempt, native) - Happy path (1 attempt, fallback) - Repair loop (3 attempts: parse fail → schema fail → success) - Max attempts exhaustion (returns Error with last attempt metadata) - Parse failure on all attempts - Schema failure on all attempts - Conversation chaining (returned conv has repair messages) - Cancellation mid-loop (BS-1 fix — token cancelled between attempt 2 and 3 returns Error Timeout, no 3rd LLM call) - Middleware hooks fired (D2 fix — Logging middleware captures structured calls) - on_repair_attempt callback invoked per attempt

docs.title: WU-3 · OpenAI native structured output — 1 day
docs.text:
UPDATED post-Oracle review (2026-06-19): Oracle flagged this as highest-risk decision. OpenAI strict mode silently rewrites schemas (forces additionalProperties: false, marks ALL properties required, converts const → enum). Without local normalization + warning, users get semantically wrong output while PAR reports success. MUST implement normalization before ship.

Files: lib/providers/openai_provider.ml, lib/providers/openai_provider.mli, new lib/core/schema_normalize.ml

Scope: - Extend build_request_body (openai_provider.ml:89-112) to accept ?response_schema:Yojson.Safe.t - New module lib/core/schema_normalize.ml — function normalize_for_openai_strict : Yojson.Safe.t -> Yojson.Safe.t * string list: 1. Add additionalProperties: false to every object subschema 2. Mark all properties as required (union with existing required) 3. Convert const: X → enum: [X] 4. Return list of transformations applied - In complete_structured: call normalizer; if transformation list non-empty, Logs.warn with summary + emit Structured_output_completed event metadata flag ("schema_normalized", \Assoc [...])for observability - When schema present (post-normalization), emitresponse_format = { type: "json_schema"; json_schema = { name = "structured_output"; schema = ; strict = true } }field - Implementcomplete_structuredwith same signature ascompleteplusresponse_schemaparameter - WireSome complete_structuredinto OpenAI provider'sllm_service` construction

Anthropic note: Anthropic output_config.format does NOT rewrite schemas the same way — no universal strict equivalent. Normalization stays OpenAI-specific.

Verification: 5+ tests in test/test_providers_structured.ml: - Request body shape (asserts response_format.json_schema.strict == true) - Schema normalization correctness (optional field → required; missing additionalProperties → added; const → enum) - Warning emission on schema mutation (capture Logs.warn output) - Graceful handling when schema absent (no response_format field) - Response parsing (canned choices[0].message.content JSON)

docs.title: WU-4 · Anthropic native output_config.format — 0.5 day
docs.text:
UPDATED 2026-06-19 post-librarian research: Anthropic GA'd native structured output (output_config.format) in 2026. The originally-planned tool_use synthesis is no longer the canonical approach.

Files: lib/providers/anthropic_provider.ml, lib/providers/anthropic_provider.mli

Scope: - Extend build_request_body (anthropic_provider.ml:109-136) to accept ?response_schema:Yojson.Safe.t - When schema present, add output_config: { format: { type: "json_schema"; schema: <user_schema> } } field to request body - Response parsing: JSON payload appears in content[0].text as a JSON-encoded string (NOT a tool_use block). Parse via Yojson.Safe.from_string on the text field. - Wire Some complete_structured into Anthropic provider's llm_service construction - Supported models (2026 GA): Opus 4.5–4.8, Sonnet 4.5–4.6, Haiku 4.5. For unsupported models, the runtime falls back to prompt-directive path (WU-5).

Verification: 3+ tests in test/test_providers_structured.ml covering request body shape (asserts output_config.format.type == "json_schema"), response parsing (canned text-block JSON), and graceful handling when schema absent.

Reference: docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/structured-outputs

docs.title: WU-5 · Generic fallback path + Mock — 0.5 day
docs.text:
UPDATED post-Oracle BS-4: Don't build schema-fuzzer for Mock. Return canned JSON, matches existing scripted_response philosophy.

Files: lib/providers/mock_provider.ml, lib/core/engine.ml (fallback closure)

Scope: - Mock provider: extend complete_structured_fn to return Some (\Assoc [])(empty object) by default, OR accept?structured_response:Yojson.Safe.tparameter atcreatetime (matches existingscripted_responsephilosophy at mock_provider.ml:53-56). Tests pass expected JSON via the param. **DO NOT synthesize from schema** — building a schema-fuzzer is overkill for tests. - **Engine fallback closure**: whencomplete_structured_fn = None, build a fallback that: 1. Prepends "You MUST respond with a valid JSON object matching this exact schema: <schema>. Do not include any text outside the JSON object." to system prompt 2. Calls existingcomplete_fn3. Returns the rawllm_response(theEngine.run_structuredloop handles parsing + validation viaJson_extract+Validation.validate_tool_input_result`)

Verification: 4+ tests using Mock provider through Runtime.invoke_structured end-to-end: - Happy path with explicit ?structured_response - Fallback path with prompt directive (use a mock that returns JSON-wrapped-in-text) - Repair loop in fallback mode - Schema validation failure in fallback mode (catches the documented limitation: array items not validated)

docs.title: WU-6 · Runtime.invoke_structured public API — 0.5 day
docs.text:
Files: lib/core/runtime.ml, lib/core/runtime.mli

Scope: - Implement Runtime.invoke_structured body mirroring Runtime.invoke (runtime.ml:335-383): - Session id generation, event bus binding - Agent lookup - Dispatch to Engine.run_structured with the right complete_fn (native or fallback) - Wrap result in structured_invoke_result, fire Llm_request_sent/Llm_response_received/new Structured_output_completed events - New event type Structured_output_completed of { attempts : int; schema_valid : bool; task_id : Task_id.t } for observability

Verification: 5+ integration tests in test/test_runtime_structured.ml covering full flow with Mock provider.

docs.title: WU-7 · Python FFI binding — 0.5 day
docs.text:
UPDATED post-Oracle BS-5: FFI error contract must match existing par_invoke — JSON status envelope, NOT NULL on error.

Files: lib/ffi/par_capi.ml, lib/ffi/par_ffi.h, lib/ffi/par_ffi.c, bindings/python/par_runtime/__init__.py, bindings/python/par_runtime/runtime.py

Scope: - New C ABI function par_invoke_structured(rt, agent_id, message, schema_json) -> char* (4 params, matching par_invoke's 3-param pattern at par_ffi.h:33) - FFI error contract (post-Oracle BS-5): always return a JSON string {"status": "ok", "value": <json>, "raw": <llm_resp>, "attempts": N} or {"status": "error", "message": "..."}. NULL only for invalid runtime handle (matches do_invoke at par_capi.ml:250-266 exactly). - Python method Runtime.invoke_structured(agent_id, message, response_schema) — accepts schema as Python dict (auto-converts to JSON via json.dumps). Returns Python dict (parsed from C JSON return). Errors raise PARError (matches existing pattern). - FFI drops ?max_repair_attempts, ?cancellation_token, ?conversation, ?on_tool_event — CONSISTENT with existing par_invoke dropping the same params. Defaults: max_repair_attempts=3, others None.

Verification: 3+ tests in bindings/python/tests/test_structured.py (unittest style, matches test_runtime.py): - Happy path (Mock provider returns valid JSON matching schema) - Schema violation (Mock returns invalid JSON, verify error) - Conversation chaining (pass conversation back via Python API — note: this requires Python-side conversation handle, may need separate FFI function)

docs.title: WU-8 · Documentation — 0.5 day
docs.text:
Files: new docs/sdk/structured-output.md, docs/index.md, README.md (Features section)

Scope: - New SDK reference: API signature, schema format, provider capabilities matrix, retry semantics, limitation note (1-level nesting) - How-to: "Get structured data from LLM" (3 examples: OpenAI native, Anthropic, fallback) - README features bullet: "Structured output via Runtime.invoke_structured — schema-validated, with native provider support"

docs.title: Not in v0.4.8 Scope
docs.text: Item Reason Defer to Nested JSON Schema validation (deep objects/arrays-of-objects) Existing validator is 1-level; extension is its own ticket v0.5 Typed 'a return variant (invoke_structured_t) Generic Yojson + caller PPX is sufficient for v1 v0.5 Streaming structured output LLM streams tokens; structured parse needs full response v0.5+ Schema-aware middleware (e.g. Structured_output middleware hook) Loop is local by design (middleware on_error cannot see conversation) v0.5 if at all Schema_violation as new retryable_condition variant Use Invalid_input + metadata ("schema_violation", \Bool true)(precedented atengine.ml:114`) v0.5 if needed jsonschema opam dependency In-tree validator sufficient for v0.4.8 surface v0.5 if nested schemas land Python-side pydantic-style schema generation Out of scope; users pass dict schemas v0.5+

docs.title: Design Decisions Log
docs.text: # Decision Date Rationale D1 Generic Yojson return, not typed 'a 2026-06-19 Avoid polymorphic-record gymnastics; PPX [%of_yojson: T] at call site covers typed use D2 Strategy B (additive complete_structured_fn field) 2026-06-19 Non-breaking for custom providers; matches existing event_bus_service pattern; capability self-declares D3 Feedback retry local to Engine, not middleware 2026-06-19 Middleware on_error cannot see conversation (signature gap at types.ml:215); middleware change would be breaking D4 1-level schema nesting only 2026-06-19 Existing validator (validation.ml:107) supports this; covers ~90% of LLM structured-output use cases; nested deferred to v0.5 D5 Anthropic via tool_use synthesis, not prompt-only 2026-06-19 Tool_use forcing is more reliable than prompt directives; matches PydanticAI pattern; reuses existing parse_content_blocks D6 Generic fallback (prompt + lenient parse) for unknown providers 2026-06-19 Don't reject providers; graceful degrade. Mock provider implements structured for tests D7 New Structured_output_completed event for observability 2026-06-19 Pattern matches existing Llm_response_received/Tool_completed events; enables /stats tracking of repair attempts D8 max_repair_attempts default = 3 2026-06-19 Industry consensus (PydanticAI: 1, OpenAI structured docs: "retry up to 3x"); 3 balances cost vs success rate

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
Document Purpose docs/STRATEGY.md §4 Priority order — type rigor is #1 docs/v0.4-ROADMAP.md §1.3 Structured output middleware (v0.4.0 — foundation this builds on) docs/v0.4.6-ROADMAP.md Predecessor — CLI bugfix patch CHANGES.md Version history docs/sdk/agent.md Existing Runtime.invoke reference (this adds sibling)

Estimated total: 6 days (5.5 days code + 0.5 day docs), parallelizable to ~3 calendar days with 3 agents in Wave 2.

Risk level: Medium. Reuses ~70% existing infrastructure (validator, conversation append helpers, provider request builders). New code is the Engine feedback loop (~150 lines) + provider-specific structured implementations (~200 lines each).

docs.title: v0.5 Roadmap
docs.text: Goal: Ship platform-true wheel for ARM64 macOS, in addition to the existing x86_64 Linux manylinux_2_28 wheel. Rationale: Current pip install par-runtime crashes on Apple Silicon Macs (loads x86_64 ELF via ctypes). v0.5.0 fixes that without forcing source-build for Mac users. Released: 2026-06-21

docs.title: v0.5.0 — Multi-arch wheel support (2 wheels shipped)
docs.text: Bump type: MINOR (new platform support = MINOR per SemVer §7; no public API change) Pre-Bump Gate answers: - (1) Public API change? No (CI/build changes only) - (2) User-visible behavior change? Yes — pip install par-runtime now works natively on ARM64 macOS, no source build needed - (3) ROADMAP expects this bump? Yes (this file)

docs.title: Deferred to v0.5.1+ (NOT in v0.5.0)
docs.text:
ARM64 Linux wheel (manylinux_2_28_aarch64): attempted via 3 paths, all blocked: 1. ubuntu-22.04-arm64 / ubuntu-24.04-arm64 GH-hosted runners — queue 45min+ on free tier, never dispatched 2. qemu-binfmt on ubuntu-22.04 host with quay.io/pypa/manylinux_2_28_aarch64:latest container — container crashes on start 3. OCaml cross-compile toolchain — Oracle-verified as not production-viable for ctypes binding linking system GMP/sqlite3

User decision 2026-06-21: skip ARM64 Linux for v0.5.0, defer to v0.5.1+. Tracked in bd issue PAR-5oq (P3). Recovery signals documented in docs/STRATEGY.md.

docs.title: Why no universal2 (Intel Mac + ARM Mac fat binary)
docs.text:
macos-13 (Intel) runner was permanently abandoned 2026-06-19 (ci.yml L16) — free-tier Intel runners queue 24h+ then hit max-execution-time. True universal2 requires BOTH macos-13 + macos-15 slices; not achievable without paid GH Actions minutes.

Trade-off accepted: - Apple Silicon Macs (the 2026 market) get a native wheel - Intel Macs (<5% of PyPI 2026 traffic) fall back to par-runtime==0.4.13 or source build - Defer Intel Mac native wheel to v0.6+ (when/if user revisits the macos-13 decision or moves to a paid tier)

docs.title: Changes from v0.4.13
docs.text:
pypi-publish.yml — refactored from single hardcoded job into a 2-job matrix: - Job build-wheel (linux-x86_64) — preserves v0.4.13 behavior (manylinux_2_28_x86_64 container + auditwheel) - Job build-wheel (macos-arm64) — NEW: macos-15 runner, delocate-wheel for dylib bundling, wheel tags --platform-tag macosx_11_0_arm64 to set the platform tag (ctypes .so gets py3-none-any by default from setuptools; filename-only rename fails PyPI 400 — internal WHEEL Tag field must match) - pypi-upload job downloads both artifacts and publishes them in one PyPI upload call

release-acceptance.yml: - Removed WHEEL_COUNT -ne 1 guard — release now ships 2 wheels - Split into accept-linux-x64 (3-container matrix: debian:12, ubuntu:22.04, ubuntu:24.04) + accept-macos-arm64 (native macos-15 runner) - Added missing actions/checkout@v4 step (macOS job was missing scripts/release-acceptance-test.py)

  • Oracle VERIFIED: all 6 checks pass

docs.title: CI iterations (8 builds: post1 → post8)
docs.text: Key fixes documented in commit history (6a12c01 → 098bf5a): 1. post1-post2: ARM64 GH runners saturated, never dispatched 2. post3-post5: qemu-binfmt container crashes on start 3. post6: dropped aarch64 job entirely (user decision) 4. post7: removed stale aarch64 download steps from upload jobs 5. post8: macos wheel rename via wheel tags (not mv — PyPI rejects filename/internal-tag mismatch)

docs.title: Out of scope for v0.5.0 (deferred)
docs.text:
• ARM64 Linux wheel: deferred to v0.5.1+ (bd PAR-5oq)
  • Intel Mac native wheel: requires macos-13 runner (rejected) OR paid GH Actions (deferred)
  • musllinux wheels (Alpine, Clear Linux): not requested; manylinux covers ≥95% of Linux PyPI installs
  • Windows wheel: ctypes on Windows requires MSVC; out of scope, source-build only on Windows
  • -manylinux_2_17 (RHEL 7 / CentOS 7): user explicitly rejected 2026-06-21 ("老的Linux我觉得完全可以不必兼容")
  • RAG Foundation (originally planned for v0.5.0 per v0.5.0-ROADMAP.md): superseded by multi-arch wheel work; RAG moved to v0.5.1+

docs.title: v0.5.0 ROADMAP
docs.text: ⚠️ SUPERSEDED 2026-06-21: This roadmap planned v0.5.0 as "RAG Foundation" (Runtime.embed, Vector_store, invoke_with_rag). Actual v0.5.0 shipped multi-arch wheel support instead (linux-x86_64 + macOS-arm64). RAG foundation is deferred to v0.5.1+. See v0.5-ROADMAP.md for what v0.5.0 actually delivered, and CHANGES.md for the release notes.

docs.title: v0.5.0 Roadmap — RAG Foundation
docs.text: Status: Draft v2. Updated 2026-06-21. Theme: Single track — RAG foundation (PAR-ngh P0). Release pipeline work moved to v0.4.11 (release engineering PATCH). Predecessor: v0.4.11 (release engineering fix). Strategic alignment: STRATEGY.md §4 priority #3 (multi-provider adapt — embeddings are LLM API surface).

docs.title: Track A (Release Pipeline) — MOVED to v0.4.11
docs.text:
The original v0.5.0 Track A (release pipeline redesign) has been moved to v0.4.11 as a PATCH release. User decision 2026-06-21: "彻底解决这些问题" within v0.4.x series.

  • Scope in v0.4.11: setuptools upgrade + ubuntu-22.04 pinning + 3-platform acceptance workflow + local dry-run script
  • Deferred to v0.5+ stretch (NOT blocking v0.5.0): manylinux container, auditwheel, ARM64, macOS wheel, OIDC auto-upload

See: - v0.4.11-ROADMAP.md — execution plan (next session) - release-pipeline-redesign.md — postmortem + scope split

docs.title: Track B: RAG Foundation (the v0.5.0 actual content)
docs.text: Source issue: PAR-ngh (P0) Strategic alignment: STRATEGY.md §4 priority #3 (multi-provider — embeddings are LLM API surface). Why now: LangChain's biggest use case. PAR's RAG gap is the single biggest "missing vs LangChain" item.

docs.title: Phase B.1 — Embedding API (Day 1-4)
docs.text:
• New Runtime.embed function: message:string -> (float array, error_category) result
  • Provider implementations:
  • openai_provider: POST /v1/embeddings with text-embedding-3-small/large
  • anthropic_provider: Anthropic has no embeddings API — route through OpenAI compat (document explicitly)
  • ollama_provider: local embeddings via nomic-embed-text
  • mock_provider: deterministic fake vectors for tests

docs.title: Phase B.2 — Vector storage interface (Day 5-7)
docs.text:
• New lib/core/vector_store.ml + .mli
  • Interface: add : documents -> unit, search : query_vector -> k:int -> result list, delete : ids -> unit
  • Backend 1: SQLite + sqlite-vec extension (zero-dependency, ships with v0.5)
  • Backend 2 (optional): External Qdrant/Milvus via HTTP (defer to v0.5.1 if scope balloons)

docs.title: Phase B.3 — Document chunking (Day 8-9)
docs.text:
• lib/core/chunking.ml:
  • chunk_by_chars : text -> max_size:int -> overlap:int -> chunk list
  • chunk_by_tokens : text -> max_tokens:int -> overlap:int -> chunk list (uses provider tokenizer if available)
  • chunk_recursive : text -> separators:string list -> chunk list (LangChain's RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter)

docs.title: Phase B.4 — RAG orchestration (Day 10-11)
docs.text:
• Runtime.invoke_with_rag:
  • Chunk user query → embed
  • Retrieve top-k from vector store
  • Build augmented system prompt with retrieved context
  • Call existing Runtime.invoke
  • New event types: Embedding_request_sent, Embedding_response_received, Retrieval_completed

docs.title: Phase B.5 — Python FFI (Day 12)
docs.text:
• Runtime.embed(message) Python method
  • Runtime.add_documents(documents, metadatas) Python method
  • 5+ pytest tests

docs.title: Phase B.6 — Documentation (Day 13)
docs.text:
• New docs/sdk/rag.md: embeddings API, vector store, chunking, retrieval patterns
  • How-to: "Build a basic RAG pipeline" (3 examples: short text, long doc, multi-doc)

GATE: User can build a working Q&A bot over a 100-page PDF using only par-runtime from PyPI (after v0.4.11 unblocks PyPI).

docs.title: Version Bump
docs.text:
• From: 0.4.11
  • To: 0.5.0-beta.YYYYMMDD → 0.5.0 stable
  • Type: MINOR (new public API: Runtime.embed, Vector_store, Runtime.invoke_with_rag)

docs.title: Definition of Done
docs.text:
• [ ] Runtime.embed works on OpenAI + Mock providers
  • [ ] Vector_store.add + Vector_store.search work on SQLite backend
  • [ ] Runtime.invoke_with_rag end-to-end Q&A over a 10-page text doc
  • [ ] Python FFI for embed + vector_store shipped
  • [ ] docs/sdk/rag.md complete with 3 examples
  • [ ] PAR-ngh closed
  • [ ] v0.4.11 already shipped (PyPI unblocked) — RAG Python tests can run via pip install

docs.title: Not in v0.5.0 Scope
docs.text:
Item Reason Defer to Function calling native (PAR-k38) Track B is bigger value v0.5.1 External vector stores (Qdrant/Milvus) SQLite-vec covers ~80% of use cases v0.5.1 Manylinux wheel + ARM64 + macOS wheel v0.4.11 covered MVP; stretch goals for portability v0.5.1 Prompt caching (PAR-4lh) Anthropic-specific, lower priority than RAG v0.5.1 Human-in-the-loop (PAR-wp0) Workflow concern, separate phase v0.5.2

Estimated total: 13 working days (Track B only, since Track A moved to v0.4.11).

docs.title: P-A-R v0.2 — 论文 + 嵌入式引擎路线图
docs.text: Created: 2026-05-28 Status: Planning

docs.title: 项目定位
docs.text:
P-A-R 是第一个在类型安全运行时中同时实现全部四个维度的 Agent Runtime：

维度 说明 Tool Result ADT handler_result 5-case sum type，编译期穷尽匹配 形式化状态机 8-state LTS，validate_transition 拒绝非法转换 表达式 DSL 资源受限求值器（深度≤10，节点≤1000） 可组合中间件 continuation-passing pipeline，5 hooks

论文叙事：λ_A (arXiv:2604.11767) 证明了类型安全 agent 组合的理论可行性（Coq 模型），但没有实现。P-A-R 是该理论的第一个完整可运行实现，并且额外提供了形式化状态机和表达式 DSL。

docs.title: 竞品矩阵
docs.text: 项目 语言 Tool ADT 状态机 DSL 中间件 可运行 λ_A (Coq 论文) Coq ❌ ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ oharness_tools Rust ✅ ❌ ❌ Observer ✅ tower-llm Rust ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ ✅ llaml OCaml 部分 ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ ocaml-ai-sdk OCaml ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ Semantic Kernel C# ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ ✅ P-A-R OCaml ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅

docs.title: 目标
docs.text: 投 ICFP 2026 Workshop 或 ML Workshop

docs.title: 论文大纲
docs.text:
"P-A-R: A Type-Safe Agent Runtime with Formal State Guarantees"

  • §1 Introduction: dynamic-typed agent frameworks 的运行时错误问题
  • §2 Background: λ_A, Shepherd (arXiv:2605.10913), CTEGs (arXiv:2604.17557)
  • §3 State Machine: 8-state LTS, validated transitions, 3 terminal states
  • §4 Expression DSL: big-step operational semantics, resource-bounded evaluation
  • §5 Middleware: continuation-passing composition, associativity proof/counterexample
  • §6 Evaluation: 111 tests + benchmark vs Python (100 tool-calling scenarios)
  • §7 Related Work (λ_A, oharness_tools, tower-llm, Shepherd, Semantic Kernel)
  • §8 Conclusion

docs.title: 关键数据点
docs.text:
• handler_result 5-case ADT vs Python any：构造 100 个工具调用场景，统计编译期捕获 vs 运行时暴露
  • validate_transition：构造 50 个非法状态转换，全部被类型系统拒绝
  • Expression evaluator：资源限制防止 DoS（深度≤10，节点≤1000）

docs.title: 关键学术引用
docs.text:
• λ_A: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.11767
  • Shepherd: https://arxiv.org/html/2605.10913
  • CTEGs: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17557
  • MCP formalization: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.24747
  • Monadic Context Engineering: https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.22431
  • Effect Systems for AI Runtimes: https://zylos.ai/research/2026-03-15-effect-systems-algebraic-effects-ai-agent-runtimes

docs.title: 2.1 工具接口与实现分离（最重要）
docs.text:
现状：tool_binding.handler 是函数类型，不可序列化。

(* 现在 *)
type tool_binding = {
  handler : Yojson.Safe.t -> cancellation_token -> handler_result;
  ...
}

(* 目标 *)
type tool_descriptor = {
  name : string;
  description : string;
  input_schema : Yojson.Safe.t;
  permission : tool_permission;
}

type tool_registry
val register : tool_registry -> descriptor:tool_descriptor ->
               handler:(Yojson.Safe.t -> cancellation_token -> handler_result) -> unit
val resolve : tool_registry -> string -> tool_binding option

收益：agent_config 可序列化，工具实现运行时注入，为 FFI 铺路。

docs.title: 2.2 Provider HTTP 代码去重
docs.text:
现状：OpenAI 和 Anthropic provider 有 80% 重复代码（URL 解析、chunked encoding、TLS 配置）。

目标：提取 lib/providers/http_client.ml 共享 HTTP 层。

docs.title: 2.3 中间件状态隔离
docs.text:
现状：retry.ml 用 ref 计数器，跨 agent 共享。

目标：per-invocation 状态，并发安全。

docs.title: 2.4 C FFI 层
docs.text:
// par_ffi.h
ParRuntime* par_init(const char* config_json);
int        par_register_tool(ParRuntime* rt, const char* name,
                              ParToolHandler handler, void* user_data);
int        par_invoke(ParRuntime* rt, const char* agent_id,
                       const char* message, char** response);
void       par_shutdown(ParRuntime* rt);

实现路径：OCaml Callback.register + Caml.startup。

docs.title: 2.5 Workflow 持久化
docs.text:
现状：Sub_workflow 和 Human_approval 是 stub（返回 error / true）。

目标：workflow checkpoint 序列化，崩溃可恢复。

docs.title: Phase 3：嵌入式验证（2 周）
docs.text: 步骤 内容 3.1 Python binding via ctypes 3.2 示例：Python 应用调用 PAR 引擎做 tool-calling 3.3 Benchmark：嵌入模式 vs 纯 Python（内存、延迟、正确性）

docs.title: 时间线
docs.text:
Week 1-2: 论文 §3-5（形式化）+ Phase 2.1-2.2（工具分离 + HTTP 去重）
Week 3:   论文 §6（benchmark）+ Phase 2.3（中间件修复）
Week 4-5: Phase 2.4-2.5（C FFI + workflow 持久化）
Week 6-7: Phase 3（Python binding + 集成验证）
Week 8:   论文收尾 + 投稿

docs.title: 优先级
docs.text: 先做 Phase 2.1 + 2.2 + 论文 §3-5，同步推进： - 工具接口分离是引擎化前提 - HTTP 去重消除技术债 - 形式化不需要改代码，可纯写

docs.title: 审计发现的现有架构弱点
docs.text: 弱点 位置 严重性 tool_binding.handler 是函数，不可序列化 types.ml:167 Critical Provider HTTP 代码 80% 重复 openai_provider.ml / anthropic_provider.ml High retry 中间件 ref 计数器非线程安全 retry.ml:53 High Sub_workflow 未实现（返回 error） workflow_engine.ml Medium Human_approval 是 stub（返回 true） workflow_engine.ml Medium 无 workflow checkpoint 序列化 workflow_engine.ml Medium 无 C FFI / 稳定 ABI 全局 Critical（引擎化 blocker） 网络栈不可注入（硬编码 Eio.Net.t） openai_provider.ml:12 High 无 phantom type / GADT types.ml 全局 Low（论文可改进）

docs.title: 现有架构优势（论文核心素材）
docs.text: 优势 位置 论文章节 handler_result 5-case ADT types.ml:55-72 §3 8-state LTS + validated transitions state_machine.ml + types.ml:269-289 §3 资源受限表达式 DSL expression.ml + types.ml:150-164 §4 Continuation-passing 中间件组合 engine.ml:7-50 §5 服务注册（module type + structural record） types.ml:489-558 §5 Opaque identifier types (Task_id, Workflow_run_id, Session_id) types.ml:7-49 §3

docs.title: Evaluation: MCP ?mcp_default_env parameter
docs.text: Status: Defer — current strict whitelist is sufficient Date: 2026-06-17

docs.title: Context
docs.text: PAR's Mcp_transport_stdio.default_child_env uses a hardcoded POSIX whitelist: HOME, LOGNAME, PATH, SHELL, TERM, USER. Users may want to pass additional environment variables to MCP servers (e.g., GITHUB_TOKEN for the GitHub MCP server).

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Keep the current strict whitelist. Do NOT add ?mcp_default_env parameter yet.

docs.title: Rationale
docs.text:
1. Security: The whitelist prevents secret leakage to untrusted npm packages. Relaxing it increases attack surface.
  2. User config already exists: The server_config's env field allows users to explicitly pass env vars per server. This is the correct, auditable approach.
  3. No user demand: No one has requested a broader default env.

docs.title: When to revisit
docs.text:
• Users report friction from having to manually specify common env vars
  • A pattern emerges of env vars that >3 MCP servers commonly need

docs.title: If implemented
docs.text:
type mcp_env_policy = [`Strict | `Inherit_all | `Inherit_named of string list]
(* In Runtime.create config: *)
mcp_default_env : mcp_env_policy  (* default: `Strict *)

docs.title: Decision: MCP tool on-demand loading
docs.text: Status: Deferred — profile real-world usage first Date: 2026-06-17

docs.title: Context
docs.text: When an MCP server exposes 100+ tools (e.g., a comprehensive GitHub MCP server), registering all tools upfront consumes memory and pollutes the LLM's tool list.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Do NOT implement on-demand loading yet. Profile real-world usage first.

docs.title: Rationale
docs.text:
1. No evidence of the problem: Current PAR users connect to MCP servers with 5-20 tools. The 100+ tool scenario is hypothetical.
  2. LLM API limits are the real bottleneck: OpenAI allows max 128 tools per request. Anthropic allows 64. On-demand loading would need to respect these limits regardless.
  3. Complexity cost: On-demand loading requires a two-phase tool discovery (list → lazy register on first reference) that adds latency to the first tool call and complexity to the engine.

docs.title: When to revisit
docs.text:
• A user connects to an MCP server with >50 tools
  • LLM tool-call accuracy degrades due to tool list bloat
  • Users report slow startup times from tool registration

docs.title: Alternative (if needed)
docs.text:
• Tool subsetting at the agent level (already supported via tools: string list option on agent_entry)
  • MCP server config could accept a tools_filter field to pre-filter which tools to register

docs.title: Decision: par_postgres opam publication strategy
docs.text: Status: Source-only for now; evaluate pgx migration for opam publication Date: 2026-06-17

docs.title: Context
docs.text: PAR ships a PostgreSQL persistence backend as a separate par_postgres opam package. Currently it's source-only (builds from the repo) because postgresql (the OCaml binding) is not in the standard opam-repository on all platforms, causing CI failures.

docs.title: Options considered
docs.text: Option Pro Con A. Source-only (current) No opam-repo dependency issues Users must build from source B. Publish to opam-repo One-command install postgresql dependency may fail on some platforms C. Migrate to pgx pgx is pure-OCaml, no C bindings, easier to publish Rewrite of persistence layer (~2 days) D. Self-host opam repo Full control, no opam-repo review delay Users need extra remote

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Keep source-only (Option A) for v0.4.x. Evaluate pgx migration (Option C) for v0.5.

docs.title: Rationale
docs.text:
1. PostgreSQL is optional: Most users use SQLite (default). PostgreSQL is for production deployments where building from source is acceptable.
  2. pgx rewrite is non-trivial: The current postgres_persistence.ml uses caqti-eio + postgresql. Migrating to pgx would require rewriting all queries (~200 lines) and testing against real PostgreSQL.
  3. CI exclusion works: The current CI excludes par_postgres from dependency resolution. This is documented in CHANGES.md v0.3.4.

docs.title: When to revisit
docs.text:
• User requests opam install par_postgres one-liner
  • pgx adds eio support (currently async/lwt only — would need an eio adapter)
  • PAR reaches v1.0 and needs production-grade opam distribution

docs.title: If implementing pgx migration
docs.text:
1. Add pgx dependency to par_postgres.opam
  2. Rewrite postgres_persistence.ml using Pgx_io (if eio adapter exists) or wrap in Lwt_eio
  3. Remove postgresql and caqti-eio from par_postgres deps
  4. Test against PostgreSQL 14+ in CI
  5. Publish to opam-repo

docs.title: ADR: par_sandbox — OS-level sandboxing for bash tool
docs.text: Status: Deferred to v0.5+ Date: 2026-06-17

docs.title: Context
docs.text: PAR's bash tool uses a type-safe ADT (Safe_command) + Policy Functor + 31-entry blacklist. This is application-level safety — no OS sandbox (bwrap, landlock, seccomp) is used.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Defer OS-level sandboxing to v0.5+ as a separate par_sandbox opam package.

docs.title: Rationale
docs.text:
1. Cross-platform complexity: bwrap is Linux-only, sandbox-exec is macOS-only. Supporting both doubles maintenance.
  2. Root requirements: bwrap requires setuid or user namespaces; not available in all containers.
  3. Current safety is adequate: The ADT + blacklist + CWD locking + env stripping + timeout + process group cleanup covers the common attack vectors for an LLM-driven bash tool.
  4. Demand not yet validated: No users have requested OS-level isolation. PAR's target users (LLM backend engineers) deploy in containers where the OS sandbox is the container runtime itself.

docs.title: Future evaluation triggers
docs.text:
• User request for stronger isolation guarantees
  • PAR adoption in multi-tenant environments
  • Regulatory compliance requirements

docs.title: Implementation plan (when triggered)
docs.text:
• par_sandbox as optional opam package
  • Linux: bwrap with --unshare-all --ro-bind / / --bind /tmp /tmp
  • macOS: sandbox-exec with a deny-by-default profile
  • Integration via Policy Functor's filter function (already pluggable)

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
English · 简体中文

Translated to English for v0.3.2.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: This document explains the internal structure of the PAR SDK. It is intended for readers who want to understand how PAR works or contribute to the core codebase.

docs.title: Core abstractions
docs.text:
PAR models the LLM agent as three layers:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     LLM Loop                             │
│  ReAct loop: observe → think → act → observe → ...      │
│  (lib/core/engine.ml)                                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Tool calls (types, dispatch, timeout, concurrency)      │
│  (lib/tools/builtin_tools.ml)                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  LLM communication (OpenAI / Anthropic)                  │
│  (lib/providers/)                                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Each layer has its own type boundary, ensuring that errors are caught at compile time.

docs.title: Module structure
docs.text:
lib/
├── core/           Types + Runtime + Engine + SDK entry point
│   ├── types.ml         All public types (agent_config, tool_descriptor, handler_result, ...)
│   ├── runtime.ml       Runtime.create / make_agent / register_tool / invoke
│   ├── engine.ml        ReAct loop implementation
│   ├── sdk.ml           Public SDK API
│   ├── tool_registry.ml Tool deduplication and registration
│   ├── cancellation.ml  Cooperative cancellation semantics
│   ├── context_manager.ml Conversation context management
│   ├── expression.ml    Expression evaluation (used by Workflow)
│   ├── state_machine.ml 8-state machine
│   └── workflow.ml      Workflow engine (sequential / parallel / conditional / map-reduce)
│
├── providers/      LLM providers
│   ├── openai_provider.ml
│   ├── anthropic_provider.ml
│   └── mock_provider.ml  (for testing)
│
├── tools/          Built-in tools (20 since v0.3.1)
│   ├── builtin_tools.ml
│   ├── bash_safe_command.ml  (v0.3.1 bash ADT)
│   ├── bash_policy.ml        (v0.3.1 safety policy)
│   └── bash_blacklist.ml     (v0.3.1 blacklist)
│
├── persistence/    Persistence backends
│   ├── sqlite_persistence.ml
│   ├── postgres_persistence.ml  (separate opam package)
│   └── persistence_writer.ml    (v0.4.1 event persistence writer)
│
├── event_bus/      Event bus (with DLQ)
│
├── middleware/     7 built-in middleware
│   ├── logging / retry / rate_limit / timeout / validation / pii_mask / sanitize_tool_output
│
├── ffi/            C FFI (par_capi.so + par_ffi.h + par_ffi.c)
│
└── par.ml          Public entry point (re-exports all sub-modules)

docs.title: Data flow: a single invoke
docs.text:
User code
  │
  ▼
Runtime.invoke agent_id "question"
  │
  ▼
Engine.execute_ReAct_loop agent conversation
  │
  ▼  ┌─→ LLM Provider (OpenAI / Anthropic) ─→ network
  │   │
  │   ◄── LLM response (text + tool_calls)
  │
  ├──→ Parse tool_calls
  │   │
  │   ▼
  │   Tool_registry.invoke tool_name
  │     │
  │     ▼
  │     Tool_handler input token → output / error
  │     │
  │     ▼
  │   Parse result, inject into conversation
  │
  ├──→ Middleware chain (logging / retry / rate_limit / ...)
  │
  ▼
Return final result (text + tool_calls history)

docs.title: Type system: why PAR is safer
docs.text:
PAR uses OCaml's strong types instead of Python-style dynamic dictionaries:

  • Tool parameter types are checked at compile time (not runtime crashes)
  • LLM response parsing uses pattern matching to force coverage of all branches
  • Configuration is validated through make_config constructors (rejects illegal values)
  • Duplicate tool names return Error (Duplicate_tool)` instead of silently overwriting

The v0.3.1 bash tool is the extreme expression of this compile-time safety: the command ADT has no Exec_raw_shell constructor — shell injection is unrepresentable at the type level.

docs.title: Concurrency model (Eio)
docs.text:
PAR's entire stack runs on Eio — OCaml 5's structured concurrency primitives.

Key points: - Every Runtime has one Eio.Switch.t (cancellation root) - Runtime.close triggers the entire switch to cancel; all fibers (tool handlers, LLM inference, SSE streams) are cancelled - cancellation_token is passed through to every tool handler; handlers can cooperatively cancel inside with_timeout - Timeouts use Eio.Fiber.first: Future.first [| timeout sleep |]

docs.title: Event stream
docs.text:
Par.Types.event is an open sum type; each event is an inline record:

type event =
  | Task_created of { task_id : Task_id.t; task_type : string; priority : int }
  | Task_completed of { task_id : Task_id.t; duration_ms : float }
  | Tool_invoked of { task_id : Task_id.t; tool_name : string }
  | Tool_progress of { task_id : Task_id.t; tool_name : string; message : string }
  | Bash_invoked of { task_id : Task_id.t; argv : string list; risk : string; ... }  (* v0.3.1 *)
  | ...
  [@@deriving yojson]

Events are emitted by the Runtime via rt.publish_event_fn, and subscribers receive them through Event_bus.subscribe. Since v0.3.0, events are also written to SQLite / Postgres (for audit and debug). As of v0.4.1, the Persistence_writer module handles event persistence with configurable retention (event_retention_seconds in runtime_config).

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Add a tool: see the 20 tools in docs/sdk/tools.md, then add one with let my_tool = { descriptor; handler } in and Runtime.register_tool.
  • Add an LLM provider: see docs/howto/custom-llm-provider.md.
  • Add middleware: see the 7 examples in lib/middleware/ and the reference at docs/sdk/middleware.md.
  • Contribute to core: read lib/core/types.ml (all public types), then follow the Runtime lifecycle through lib/core/runtime.ml.

docs.title: Concurrency
docs.text:
English · 简体中文

Translated to English for v0.3.2.

docs.title: How-to: Concurrency Patterns
docs.text: PAR has three concurrency layers, from the outside in:

docs.title: 1. Multiple agents in parallel
docs.text:
The outermost layer — independent agent tasks running simultaneously:

Eio.Switch.run (fun sw ->
  let rt = Runtime.create ~config ... sw in
  (* Launch 3 independent agents *)
  let results = Eio.Fiber.all [
    fun () -> Runtime.invoke rt "agent-a" "task 1";
    fun () -> Runtime.invoke rt "agent-b" "task 2";
    fun () -> Runtime.invoke rt "agent-c" "task 3";
  ] in
  (* results is [(result_a, error_b, result_c) ...] *)
  List.iter (function
    | Ok r -> Printf.printf "OK: %s\n" r
    | Error e -> Printf.printf "FAIL: %s\n" (error_to_string e)) results
)

Note: Eio.Fiber.all is structured concurrency — if one fiber fails, the switch cancels all of them. If you want fire-and-forget isolation, use Eio.Fiber.fork and manage each lifecycle independently.

docs.title: 2. Multiple invokes on the same agent
docs.text:
If the same agent receives multiple independent questions (e.g. batch processing):

let inputs = ["question 1"; "question 2"; ...; "question N"] in
let tasks = List.map (fun input ->
  fun () -> Runtime.invoke rt agent_id input
) inputs in
let results = Eio.Fiber.all tasks in

Runtime has an internal task_semaphore (runtime_config.default_quota.max_concurrent_tasks) that automatically throttles to prevent overload.

docs.title: 3. Parallel tool calls (v0.3.0+)
docs.text:
When the LLM returns multiple tool_calls in a single response (e.g. "search then fetch"), PAR executes them in parallel by default:

(* runtime_config.parallel_tool_execution = true (default) *)
(* One LLM response triggers concurrent tool calls, then results are collected serially *)

Disable it (if tool calls in the LLM response have dependencies):

let config = {
  ...
  parallel_tool_execution = false;  (* serial tool execution *)
  ...
}

Fine-grained control — set concurrency_limit per tool:

let my_tool = { descriptor = {
  name = "expensive_db_query";
  ...
  concurrency_limit = Some 2;  (* max 2 concurrent invocations *)
}; handler = ... } in
Runtime.register_tool rt my_tool;

docs.title: 4. Bash tool concurrency (v0.3.1)
docs.text:
The bash tool defaults to concurrency_limit = Some 4 (max 4 concurrent bash calls per agent). This is intentional in v0.3.1 — bash subprocesses are heavy, and 4 is enough for typical LLM workflow parallelism.

Added in v0.3.1: the sandboxed_path abstraction ensures different bash calls do not conflict on cwd.

docs.title: 5. Rate limiting
docs.text:
lib/middleware/rate_limit.ml provides token-bucket rate limiting:

(* Global: max 60 LLM calls per second *)
let config = {
  ...
  middleware = [Par_middleware.rate_limit ~max_requests:60 ~window_seconds:1.0 ()];
}

Or per-agent:

let agent = {
  ...
  middleware = [Par_middleware.rate_limit ~max_requests:10 ~window_seconds:1.0 ()];
}

docs.title: 6. Concurrency inside workflows
docs.text:
lib/core/workflow.ml supports sequential / parallel / map-reduce steps:

let workflow = {
  id = "fan-out-fan-in";
  steps = [
    Parallel [
      Tool_call { tool_name = "fetch_url"; input = `Assoc [...url_a...] };
      Tool_call { tool_name = "fetch_url"; input = `Assoc [...url_b...] };
      Tool_call { tool_name = "fetch_url"; input = `Assoc [...url_c...] };
    ];
    Map_reduce {
      over = Expression.Variable "results";
      step = Tool_call { tool_name = "summarize"; input = ... };
      reduce = Expression.Call (Variable "concat", [Variable "acc"; Variable "item"]);
    };
  ];
}

Parallel runs concurrently, Map_reduce reduces serially. See docs/sdk/workflow.md for details.

docs.title: Anti-patterns
docs.text:
• Do not use Thread.create inside tool handlers — it escapes Eio switch cancellation
  • Do not use Lwt or Async — PAR's entire stack is Eio; mixing in Lwt will deadlock
  • Do not set concurrency_limit = None (unless you are 100% sure there is no risk) — OOM hazard

docs.title: Custom LLM Provider
docs.text:
English · 简体中文

Translated to English for v0.3.2.

docs.title: How-to: Register a Custom LLM Provider
docs.text: PAR ships OpenAI and Anthropic providers out of the box. This guide shows how to add another one (e.g. Cohere, Mistral, self-hosted Ollama, etc.).

docs.title: Step 1: Find the llm_provider_config type
docs.text:
lib/core/types.ml defines llm_provider_config:

type llm_provider_config =
  | Openai of { api_key : string; base_url : string option; ... }
  | Anthropic of { api_key : string; base_url : string option; ... }
  | Ollama of { base_url : string; model : string }
  | Custom of {
      name : string;        (* your provider name *)
      base_url : string;     (* HTTP endpoint *)
      request_fn : ...       (* custom request/response function *)
    }
[@@deriving yojson]

docs.title: Step 2: Implement the llm_service interface
docs.text:
Every provider must implement the llm_service record type (lib/core/types.ml):

type llm_service = {
  complete_fn : ... -> (string, error_category) result;
  stream_fn : ... -> (string, error_category) result;
  close_fn : unit -> unit;
}

See lib/providers/openai_provider.ml:1-50 — copy the pattern and change the HTTP endpoint and request/response schema.

docs.title: Step 3: Register in make_runtime
docs.text:
let () =
